categoryName: Antifungals: Drugs to Treat Fungal Infections

questions.topicName: Antifungals: Drugs to Treat Fungal Infections
questions.stem: This diagram maps where different antifungal classes act on the fungal cell. The class that inserts into the membrane and binds ergosterol produces which primary effect?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Inhibition of beta-glucan synthase in the cell wall

choices.label: B
choices.text: Blockade of the enzyme that converts lanosterol to ergosterol

choices.label: C
choices.text: Disruption of microtubule function during mitosis

choices.label: D
choices.text: Formation of pores that increase membrane permeability and cause leakage of cell contents

choices.label: E
choices.text: Incorporation into fungal DNA and RNA to halt synthesis

explanation.summary: Polyenes such as amphotericin B bind ergosterol and create pores in the fungal membrane, increasing permeability and causing lethal leakage of intracellular contents. The other options correspond to azoles, echinocandins, griseofulvin, and flucytosine respectively.
explanation.whyCorrect: Polyenes such as amphotericin B bind ergosterol and create pores in the fungal membrane, increasing permeability and causing lethal leakage of intracellular contents. The other options correspond to azoles, echinocandins, griseofulvin, and flucytosine respectively.

questions.topicName: Antifungals: Drugs to Treat Fungal Infections
questions.stem: This chart maps antifungal adverse effects to organ systems. Amphotericin B appears in the nephrotoxicity panel; what is the principal mechanism of its renal injury?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Osmotic diuresis from unmetabolized drug in the filtrate

choices.label: B
choices.text: Immune-complex deposition in the glomerular basement membrane

choices.label: C
choices.text: Inhibition of CYP450 enzymes within renal tubular cells

choices.label: D
choices.text: Renal vasoconstriction with direct tubular toxicity reducing perfusion

choices.label: E
choices.text: Crystal precipitation obstructing the renal tubules

explanation.summary: Amphotericin B causes decreased renal perfusion via vasoconstriction plus direct tubular damage, leading to renal tubular acidosis, potassium and magnesium wasting, and reduced erythropoietin (anemia). The distractors describe mechanisms not responsible for its nephrotoxicity.
explanation.whyCorrect: Amphotericin B causes decreased renal perfusion via vasoconstriction plus direct tubular damage, leading to renal tubular acidosis, potassium and magnesium wasting, and reduced erythropoietin (anemia). The distractors describe mechanisms not responsible for its nephrotoxicity.

questions.topicName: Antifungals: Drugs to Treat Fungal Infections
questions.stem: This schematic depicts a fungal plasma membrane, with the green molecules embedded among the blue phospholipids. What is the clinical significance of these green sterol molecules?

choices.label: A
choices.text: They are the selective target of antifungal drugs because they are absent from human membranes

choices.label: B
choices.text: They serve as the receptor for host antibodies against fungi

choices.label: C
choices.text: They are the site where beta-glucan is polymerized into the cell wall

choices.label: D
choices.text: They actively pump antifungal drugs out of the cell

choices.label: E
choices.text: They generate the aerobic energy the fungal cell requires

explanation.summary: The green molecules are ergosterol, the fungal analog of cholesterol. Because ergosterol is unique to fungal membranes and absent in humans, it (and its synthesis) is exploited for selective toxicity by polyenes and azoles.
explanation.whyCorrect: The green molecules are ergosterol, the fungal analog of cholesterol. Because ergosterol is unique to fungal membranes and absent in humans, it (and its synthesis) is exploited for selective toxicity by polyenes and azoles.
